seoTitle: Visualize a Page Tree in Confluence: 2026 Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to visualize a page tree in Confluence in 2026 using the Page Tree macro, filter by root page, and embed a live navigation directory.
intro: Hunting through flat, disconnected document clusters wastes time when the underlying structure isn't visible. This guide shows how to display an organized page hierarchy in Confluence in 2026 using the Page Tree macro.

steps.heading: Open the page tree macro
steps.body: In edit mode, type `/page tree` and hit Enter to drop the dynamic hierarchy block.

steps.heading: Open macro settings
steps.body: Click the inserted macro container to access the advanced floating configuration panel.

steps.heading: Set the root
steps.body: Choose to show your entire space library, or isolate an independent parent page as the starting root.

steps.heading: Toggle display options
steps.body: Check the boxes to include a search bar or show expand and collapse buttons.

steps.heading: Set sort order
steps.body: Choose to arrange sub-pages alphabetically or by created date.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Review your navigation structure map and click Publish to go live.

faq.question: Does the page tree update automatically as pages are added?
faq.answer: Yes, the navigation engine tracks structural changes in real time and reflects folder updates automatically.

faq.question: Can I show just one branch instead of the whole space?
faq.answer: Yes, the root parameters let you isolate an independent parent page instead of showing the entire space library.

faq.question: Can I add a search bar to the page tree?
faq.answer: Yes, toggle layout filters let you include an integrated search bar alongside the hierarchy.

faq.question: Can I embed a miniature page tree in a sidebar?
faq.answer: Yes, you can embed a smaller page directory inside a sidebar panel or a team homepage slot.
